extraction.one_line_summary: HODEG Álvaro José won the sprint after Quickstep took control at 3km, with MØRKØV delivering the leadout through final 800m.
tactical_patterns: Quickstep deployed three-rider train (JUNGELS-MØRKØV-HODEG) from 3km out, overwhelming teams that waited for traditional final kilometer leadouts.
MOVISTAR set early pace then surrendered to Quickstep at 3km, revealing their tactical hierarchy prioritizes GC over stage hunting.
BORA's leadout with BENNETT Sam and MCCARTHY Jay collapsed inside 1km when attacking Quickstep's established train rather than positioning earlier.
BOUHANNI Nacer wheel-surfed without teammates, confirming Cofidis lacks organized sprint infrastructure even on flat finishes.

rider_signals.HODEG Álvaro José: positive: explosive finishing speed from third wheel confirms he can win even without perfect positioning
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: positive: held 60kph leadout through 800m, demonstrates sustained power differentiates elite from competent leadout men
rider_signals.JUNGELS Bob: positive: executed transition role at 800m after pulling from 3km, showing dual-threat domestique capacity
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: BORA's leadout disintegrated when positioned too far back entering final kilometer against organized Quickstep train
rider_signals.BOUHANNI Nacer: negative: isolated without leadout support, suggests team selection or commitment issues rather than form deficit
rider_signals.BONIFAZIO Niccolò: neutral: positioned fourth wheel but unable to come around HODEG, confirms top-10 sprint capability without top-5 acceleration
